prompt:
Our workflow documents get edited by hand and by three different tools, so no two files spell the same thing the same way: durations in odd units, key order scrambled, then omitted here and spelled out there. I want a schema package that loads a document into honest Go types, NORMALIZES it, and can re-emit one canonical form — so our formatter is just Parse|Dump and diffs stop lying. Package flowschema, module go-flowschema. gopkg.in/yaml.v3 v3.0.1 is already pinned in go.mod and go.sum — run go mod download once; leave go.mod, go.sum, and the test file alone.

Exports (single file flowschema.go is fine):

- Parse(data []byte) (*Workflow, error)
- Dump(wf *Workflow) string
- Types: Workflow{Document Document; Timeout *Timeout; Do []TaskEntry}; Document{DSL, Namespace, Name, Version string}; Timeout{After time.Duration}; TaskEntry{Name string; Task Task}; Task{Kind, If, Then string; Timeout *Timeout; Call *CallSpec; Set map[string]string; Switch []Case; For *ForSpec; Wait time.Duration}; CallSpec{Fn string; With map[string]string}; Case{When, Then string}; ForSpec{Each, In, At string; Do []TaskEntry}.

The document shape (the subset these tools emit): top level document / timeout (optional) / do. document has dsl, namespace, name, version (optional). do is a non-empty list of task entries; each entry is a mapping with EXACTLY ONE key, the task name, whose value is the task body — you'll want custom yaml.v3 UnmarshalYAML on *yaml.Node for that, tags alone can't express it. A body declares exactly one task type among call, set, switch, for, wait (Kind records which): call's value is the function name (a with mapping of string pairs may accompany it — only on call tasks); set's value is a string-to-string mapping; switch is a list of cases {when?, then?} where a case without when is the default; for has each, in, optional at, and a nested do list (recurse; same entry rules); wait's value is a duration. Common fields on any task: if (expression string), then (task name or continue/end), timeout {after: duration}. Jump-target existence is NOT this package's concern (the linter owns that) — but shape is.

Durations are ISO-8601 time strings: PT then hours/minutes/seconds components, in order, at least one, digits only (PT90S, PT1H30M, PT10M). Parse to time.Duration; zero or negative or anything else (P1D, 10s, PT) is invalid. On output, re-render MAXIMAL: PT90S becomes PT1M30S, PT3600S becomes PT1H, PT120M becomes PT2H.

Normalization on parse: every task and every switch case with no then gets then: continue. Dump always writes then.

Errors: return the first problem, message prefixed line N: using the yaml.Node line of the offending construct. Exact texts (the tests match with Contains): line N: task entry must be a mapping with exactly one key — line N: task must declare exactly one task type (found none) — ... (found call, set) with the type keys alphabetical, comma-space — line N: "with" is only valid on call tasks (at the with key) — line N: invalid ISO-8601 duration "P1D" (at the value) — line N: document.dsl must be the string "1.0" — line N: unknown task field "retries" — line N: unknown document field "owner". dsl must be the yaml STRING "1.0": an unquoted 1.0 is a float and must be rejected with that same dsl message (check the node's tag, don't let it coerce), as must a missing document. A missing or empty do is do must be a non-empty list.

Dump is hand-rolled and deterministic — do NOT use yaml.Marshal, it won't give you this layout. Two-space indent; list dashes indented two past the parent key; entry fields two past the entry name. Order: document (dsl always double-quoted, namespace, name, version only if set), timeout if set, do. Task body: the type key first (call with its with mapping after it, keys sorted; set keys sorted), then if (only if set), timeout (only if set), then (always, last). Case: when (omitted for the default), then. for: each, in, at (only if set), do. Every scalar except dsl is written plain — our documents only use values that read back unchanged (this is a stated simplification, don't fight it). One concrete example, byte for byte — this input:

do:
  - fetch:
      then: continue
      with:
        method: GET
        endpoint: /orders
      call: http
      timeout:
        after: PT90S
document:
  name: intake
  dsl: "1.0"
  namespace: ops

must Dump as:

document:
  dsl: "1.0"
  namespace: ops
  name: intake
do:
  - fetch:
      call: http
      with:
        endpoint: /orders
        method: GET
      timeout:
        after: PT1M30S
      then: continue

and Dump then Parse then Dump must be a fixpoint (the emitted YAML re-parses to the same canonical bytes). go test -race ./... is the gate; flowschema_test.go stays as it is.
